stories.headline: Our View of Worth Shapes How We Age
stories.summary: The article argues that believers should see aging as part of God’s eternal story rather than mere decline. It urges people at every stage to adopt a God‑centered narrative of growing older.

stories.headline: Paul vs. Stoicism: A Theological Showdown
stories.summary: The piece notes that Stoic ideas echo biblical truths but collapse when faced with mortality. It concludes that the apostle’s teachings ultimately surpass the philosophy’s limited scope.

stories.headline: Nativ Brings Local AI to macOS
stories.summary: Developer Prince Canuma bundles the MLX‑VLM library into a desktop app that lets Mac users run vision‑LLMs offline. The tool mirrors LM Studio’s capabilities while leveraging Apple’s MLX framework.

stories.headline: GOP Slams Smithsonian Over ‘Oppressor’ Narrative
stories.summary: In a House subcommittee hearing, Republicans blasted the National Museum of American History director after a White House report flagged bias. They claim the museum prioritizes a divisive agenda over balanced history.

stories.headline: Anthropic’s Claude Code Team Talks Security and Tool Design
stories.summary: Simon Willison recapped a fireside chat with Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar covering Claude Code, Claude Tag, Fable, and coding‑agent security. The discussion also revealed how Anthropic uses these tools internally.

stories.headline: Anthropic Clears $1.5 B Copyright Settlement Over Book Piracy
stories.summary: A federal judge approved a $1.5 billion settlement after Anthropic was found to have used scanned books without permission to train Claude. The deal aims to resolve the copyright claims and set a precedent for AI training data.

stories.headline: Apple Publishes SoC 3 Audit for Private Cloud Compute
stories.summary: Apple released detailed audit documentation confirming compliance of its Private Cloud Compute SoC 3 chip. The reports provide transparency for enterprise customers seeking secure, on‑premise AI workloads.

stories.headline: Redeeming the Legacy of Bad Fathers in Scripture
stories.summary: The article examines how biblical narratives acknowledge fathers who fail yet offer routes to redemption. It stresses that while the past cannot be erased, God can transform its consequences.

stories.headline: Zelensky Dismisses Top General Amid Public Outcry
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky removed Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi after protests over the removal of Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. The decision aims to quell unrest and restore confidence in Ukraine’s military leadership.

stories.headline: Zelensky Appoints New Army Chief Following Syrskyi’s Ouster
stories.summary: Following weeks of demonstrations, Zelensky named Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new commander‑in‑chief of Ukraine’s armed forces. The reshuffle is intended to stabilize leadership and address public dissatisfaction.

stories.headline: Nicaragua's President Ortega Extends His 20-Year Rule
stories.summary: Nicaragua's President Daniel Ortega has announced that the country will not hold elections in 2027, effectively extending his rule. This move has sparked concerns about the erosion of democracy in the Central American nation.

stories.headline: European Commission Dictates Sweeping AI Interoperability Rules to Google
stories.summary: The European Commission has issued guidance to Google on AI interoperability and search sharing, imposing significant changes on the tech giant. The scope of the requirements has left many stunned.

stories.headline: Kimi K3 AI Model Matches Fable's Performance
stories.summary: Researchers have found that the Kimi K3 AI model is competitive with Fable, a state-of-the-art language model. This breakthrough has significant implications for the field of natural language processing.

stories.headline: AI Models Create Impressive Drawings of the Mona Lisa
stories.summary: Researchers have used various AI models, including GPT-5.6 and Claude, to create stunning drawings of the Mona Lisa. The results showcase the capabilities of AI in generating artistic content.

stories.headline: US Launches Fresh Strikes on Iran Amid Warning of Retaliation
stories.summary: The US has launched new strikes on Iran, prompting a warning from former President Donald Trump of potential retaliation. Iran has responded by hitting two ships in the Strait of Hormuz and targeting sites in Bahrain and Jordan.

stories.headline: Israeli Strikes in Gaza Claim 12 Lives, Including Family of Six
stories.summary: A series of Israeli strikes in Gaza has resulted in the deaths of 12 people, including a family of six. The Israeli military claims the targets were Hamas operatives, but the humanitarian toll is mounting.

stories.headline: OpenAI and Hugging Face Address Security Incident
stories.summary: OpenAI and Hugging Face have disclosed a security incident during model evaluation, sparking concerns about the safety of AI systems. The companies have taken steps to address the issue and prevent similar incidents in the future.

stories.headline: Trump Imposes 50% Tariffs on Canadian Goods
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, citing unfair trade practices. The move is likely to escalate tensions between the two nations and have far-reaching economic implications.

stories.headline: Jack Dorsey Launches Buzz, a New Platform Combining Team Chat and AI
stories.summary: Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey has launched Buzz, a new platform that integrates team chat, AI agents, and Git hosting. The platform aims to revolutionize the way teams collaborate and work together.

stories.headline: Trip-Hop Icon's Latest Album Explores Parental Grief
stories.summary: The latest album from a trip-hop icon is a stylistically uneven but emotionally charged exploration of parental grief. The artist's brooding, desolate songs showcase their creative energy and vulnerability.

stories.headline: Caribenya Expands the Boundaries of Caribbean Music
stories.summary: Caribenya's latest album showcases her unique blend of focused songwriting and vanguardist style, pushing the possibilities of what she calls 'the Caribbean sublime.' Her music is a testament to the diversity and richness of Caribbean culture.

stories.headline: Lebanese Army Deploys in 'Pilot Zone' After Israeli Withdrawal
stories.summary: A US-brokered agreement has led to the withdrawal of Israeli troops from a small part of Lebanon, paving the way for the deployment of Lebanese army troops in the area. The move is seen as a step towards stability in the region.

stories.headline: Computable Revolutionizes GPU Trading with Flexible Leasing Model
stories.summary: Computable, a startup founded by former Jump Trading and Coinbase executives, has launched a platform that allows users to buy, sell, and redeem GPUs for specific periods of time. This innovative approach aims to disrupt the traditional GPU leasing market.

stories.headline: Sweet Thief's Dreamy Folk-Pop is a Delicate, Detailed Treat
stories.summary: The Australian duo Sweet Thief has created a captivating sound that blends dreamy folk-pop and doo wop elements, resulting in a unique and charming musical experience. Their songs are like intricate, miniature works of art.

stories.headline: Bologna Protests Erupt After Man Dies in Police Restraint
stories.summary: Thousands of people took to the streets in Bologna to demand justice for a Moroccan-born man who died while being restrained by police. The protests highlight concerns about police brutality and accountability.

stories.headline: India's Cockroach Movement Vows to Continue Protests Despite Crackdown
stories.summary: The Cockroach movement, a satirical protest that has evolved into a nationwide challenge to India's government, has vowed to continue its protests despite a police crackdown. The movement is led by young people who are dissatisfied with the current state of affairs.

stories.headline: Houthis Announce Blockade, FDA Investigates Cyclospora Outbreak
stories.summary: The Iran-backed Houthis have announced a blockade of a waterway on the Red Sea, while the FDA continues to investigate the source of a Cyclospora outbreak linked to Taylor Farms lettuce. The FDA has retracted a test that initially identified the lettuce as the source of the outbreak.

stories.headline: EU Court Rules VPNs are Lawful Technical Tools
stories.summary: In a landmark ruling, the EU Court has declared that VPNs are lawful technical tools, affirming the right to online privacy and security. The decision has significant implications for internet users and online freedom.

stories.headline: Defense Secretary Seeks Nearly $90B Funding Boost Amid Iran Tensions
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will appear on Capitol Hill to request a nearly $90 billion funding boost, citing the ongoing tensions with Iran as a key reason for the increased spending. The request is part of the government's efforts to address national security concerns.

stories.headline: Ghanaian Politician Sentenced to 20 Years for Illegal Mining
stories.summary: Bernard Antwi Boasiako, a top Ghanaian politician, has been sentenced to 20 years in prison and fined over $10,000 for his involvement in illegal mining. The sentence has sparked shock and anger in Ghana, with many calling for greater accountability in the country's leadership.

stories.headline: Space Economy Sim in Rust and Bevy: A Self-Running Galactic Marketplace
stories.summary: A space-themed simulation economy built with Rust and Bevy, where autonomous ships navigate trade routes, refuel, and retrofit without scripted instructions. This self-running economy simulates a complex, dynamic market with hundreds of ships making their own decisions.

stories.headline: France to Block Non-Quantum-Secure Products from Certification in 2027
stories.summary: France's Anssi certification body will no longer approve products without post-quantum cryptography starting in 2027, as the country prepares for the potential risks of quantum computing. This move aims to ensure the security of French products in the face of emerging quantum threats.

stories.headline: Furtex: A Linux Rootkit and Evasion Research Toolkit
stories.summary: Furtex is an open-source toolkit for Linux post-exploitation, rootkit development, and evasion research, providing a comprehensive framework for security researchers and developers. This tool aims to facilitate the exploration of advanced threat techniques and improve the security of Linux systems.

stories.headline: BLING SLIME VØL 1: Atlanta Rapper's Latest Release
stories.summary: The Atlanta rapper and ØWay figurehead has released a new album filled with acrobatic punch-ins and glitzy production, showcasing his skills as a rising emcee in the city's hip-hop scene. This release marks a significant step in the artist's career, solidifying his reputation as a talented rapper.

stories.headline: Canada Hit with 50% Tariffs by Trump, Escalating Trade Tensions
stories.summary: The US has imposed a 50% tariff on Canadian goods, marking a significant escalation in trade tensions between the two North American neighbors. This move is likely to have far-reaching consequences for the Canadian economy and its trade relationships with the US.

stories.headline: Gemini AI Models: Deprecation of Temperature, Top_P, and Top_K Parameters
stories.summary: The Gemini AI API has deprecated and ignored the temperature, top_p, and top_k parameters in its latest models, as the AI landscape continues to evolve and improve. This change aims to simplify the API and improve the overall performance of the Gemini AI models.

stories.headline: A Digestion of the Jacobian Conjecture Counterexample
stories.summary: A recent blog post provides an in-depth analysis of the Jacobian conjecture counterexample, a significant development in the field of algebraic geometry. This counterexample has far-reaching implications for our understanding of polynomial rings and their properties.

stories.headline: Two French Firefighters Die in Blaze Near Bordeaux Airport
stories.summary: A brush fire near Bordeaux-Mérignac Airport has claimed the lives of two French firefighters, highlighting the risks and challenges faced by emergency responders in tackling complex blazes. The investigation into the cause of the fire is ongoing.

stories.headline: Brazil's Amazon Rainforest Sees Sharp Decline in Burned Area
stories.summary: The amount of land burned in the Amazon rainforest has decreased significantly in 2025, marking a positive trend in the conservation of the world's largest rainforest. This decline is attributed to a combination of factors, including improved land management and conservation efforts.

stories.headline: FreeInk: An Open Ecosystem for E-Readers
stories.summary: FreeInk is an open-source ecosystem for e-readers, providing a platform for developers to create custom e-reader experiences and apps. This initiative aims to promote innovation and competition in the e-reader market, benefiting consumers and developers alike.

stories.headline: Bloomy Seeks Founding Engineer to Shape Its Future
stories.summary: Bloomy, a Y Combinator-backed startup, is looking for a founding engineer to help build its team and drive innovation. This role offers a chance to be part of a company's early stages and shape its technical direction.

stories.headline: Roblox Secures Android Devices with GrapheneOS Support
stories.summary: Roblox has officially added support for GrapheneOS, a secure Android operating system, to enhance the security of its users' devices. This move aims to provide a safer gaming experience for Roblox users.

stories.headline: NPR's Top Games of 2026 So Far
stories.summary: NPR's staff has picked the best games of the first half of 2026, featuring blockbusters like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert, as well as early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2.

stories.headline: Avocado Oil Claims Exposed as Misleading
stories.summary: A new study reveals that many foods advertised as made with 100% avocado oil are likely not what consumers think they're buying, highlighting the need for greater transparency in food labeling.

stories.headline: Army Veteran Arrested for Manhattan Federal Building Fire
stories.summary: Andrew Arrabaca, a 43-year-old army veteran, has been arrested for allegedly starting a fire outside a Manhattan federal building, armed with various weapons and fireworks.

stories.headline: India's 'Cockroach' Protest Continues Amid Police Crackdown
stories.summary: Protesters in Delhi continue to demonstrate against the government, despite a police crackdown that left at least 60 protesters wounded and 118 police officers injured.

stories.headline: Police Officers Arrested for Roadside Bribe in Nigeria
stories.summary: Two police officers have been arrested for allegedly stopping Nigeria's anti-corruption boss at a fake roadblock and demanding a bribe, highlighting corruption within the police force.

stories.headline: Ferry Capsizes Off Guyana, Leaving Dozens Dead or Missing
stories.summary: At least 41 people have died after a ferry capsized off the coast of Guyana, with 77 rescued and dozens still missing, sparking a search and rescue operation.

stories.headline: A La Carte: Insights and Reflections from Tim Challies
stories.summary: Tim Challies' latest A La Carte features a collection of articles on marriage, prayer, Christian unity, and more, offering insights and reflections on various aspects of faith and life.

stories.headline: Explore the World's Great Castles and Fortresses with This Interactive Map
stories.summary: The Castle Map is an interactive resource that showcases the world's most impressive castles and fortresses, providing a unique glimpse into history and architecture.
